behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: This failure is about module handoff (hook event payload -> segmentation -> classifier input), not just isolated parser logic. Integration is sufficient because behavior text only requires handling a command string with newlines; it does not require real subprocess/OS/network or any AI output. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified by the behavior text focusing on segmentation semantics only, with no real infra dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run the PostToolUse command-processing path with a multiline command payload in a temp test context and assert the produced segments are split per newline statement boundaries before classification.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: The incident came from interaction between segmentation and trigger detection across modules. A realistic in-process integration test catches this; no real `gh` subprocess call is required by the behavior text. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because the behavior says 'is recognized as a PR-create trigger' (classification), not real CLI execution; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI model decisions are involved.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the hook’s end-to-end classification path with a multiline block containing assignments then `gh pr create`, and assert final classification is PR-create trigger (not `not_a_trigger`).

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: This is the negative-control counterpart of behavior 2 and still depends on segmentation + trigger matcher interaction. Integration is the lowest level that validates that composed behavior. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified by behavior text requiring only trigger classification correctness, not real infrastructure side effects; Agentic/Workflow are not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Run the same integrated command-processing path with multiline assignments ending in `gh issue create` and assert classification is non-PR trigger.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: This requires wiring detection output to identifier reconstruction and state persistence, crossing module boundaries and local filesystem I/O. Integration covers this with temp files. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because behavior text does not require real external API/subprocess round-trip for ID derivation, only reconstruction and persistence; Agentic/Workflow are not involved.
behaviors.test_description: Given a detected PR-create event fixture, run hook persistence flow in a temp state directory and assert stored state includes the reconstructed PR identifier in the expected field.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: This is an inter-module contract test (PostToolUse writer -> PreToolUse reader/enforcer) with local filesystem round-trip, which is exactly Integration scope. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because the behavior text limits visibility to internal hook-to-hook state handoff on local state, not external service/API/UI boundaries; Agentic/Workflow are not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Run PostToolUse to write gate state, then invoke PreToolUse enforcement against the same temp state store and assert enforcement behavior reflects the written state.
